0	package agent
1	
2	// service_run.go provides Runner-free helpers that drive every operation via
3	// the agentlib.FizeauService service surface (CONTRACT-003). RunWithConfigViaService
4	// is the only run entry point; legacy RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith and the
5	// runFixtureHarnessViaRunner shim were retired in B22d-h.
6	
7	import (
8		"context"
9		"fmt"
10		"os"
11		"strings"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
15		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"
16	)
17	
18	// serviceRunFactory, when non-nil, overrides NewServiceFromWorkDir inside
19	// RunWithConfigViaService. CLI-level integration tests inject a stub to
20	// observe ServiceExecuteRequest fields without a real agent server.
21	var serviceRunFactory func(workDir string) (agentlib.FizeauService, error)
22	
23	// SetServiceRunFactory installs a service factory for RunWithConfigViaService.
24	// Pass nil to restore production behavior. Exported for cmd/ integration tests.
25	//
26	// Production reachability: invoked from init() with nil so the symbol is
27	// reachable from main() under deadcode RTA. Real overrides come from cmd/
28	// integration tests at test-setup time.
29	func SetServiceRunFactory(f func(workDir string) (agentlib.FizeauService, error)) {
30		serviceRunFactory = f
31	}
32	
33	func init() {
34		// Reach SetServiceRunFactory from main() so it survives deadcode RTA.
35		// The nil reset is a no-op: production never sets the factory.
36		SetServiceRunFactory(nil)
37	}
38	
39	// appendProviderTimeoutHint appends a configuration override hint to errMsg
40	// when it indicates a provider request timeout fired. Helps operators find
41	// the knob to adjust (AC4 of ddx-2c63bb95).
42	func appendProviderTimeoutHint(errMsg string, providerTimeout time.Duration) string {
43		if errMsg == "" || !strings.Contains(errMsg, "provider request timeout") {
44			return errMsg
45		}
46		return errMsg + fmt.Sprintf(
47			"; request_timeout=%s exceeded — override via"+
48				" agent.endpoints.<name>.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml"+
49				" or pass --request-timeout DURATION to execute-bead/execute-loop",
50			providerTimeout.Round(time.Second),
51		)
52	}
53	
54	// RunWithConfigViaService dispatches a single agent invocation through the
55	// agent service. It takes a sealed ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and an
56	// AgentRunRuntime (per-invocation plumbing/intent), and is the SD-024
57	// successor to the retired RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith entry points.
58	//
59	// The virtual and script harnesses route through a DDx-owned Runner path,
60	// not the upstream service. These are not "carve-outs pending migration" —
61	// they are different products from upstream's same-named stubs. DDx's
62	// virtual is a content-addressed record/replay dictionary keyed by
63	// PromptHash; upstream's is a unit-test stub where callers stuff
64	// virtual.response into Metadata. DDx's script reads a filesystem directive
65	// file; upstream does not model this at all.
66	func RunWithConfigViaService(ctx context.Context, workDir string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
67		if ctx == nil {
68			ctx = context.Background()
69		}
70	
71		harness := rcfg.Passthrough().Harness
72	
73		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
74			sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
75			if sessionLogDir == "" {
76				sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
77			}
78			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(workDir, "")}
79			r := NewRunner(cfg)
80			r.WorkDir = workDir
81			return r.runInternal(RunArgs{
82				Context:       ctx,
83				Harness:       rcfg.Harness(),
84				Prompt:        runtime.Prompt,
85				PromptFile:    runtime.PromptFile,
86				PromptSource:  runtime.PromptSource,
87				Correlation:   runtime.Correlation,
88				Model:         rcfg.Model(),
89				Provider:      rcfg.Provider(),
90				ModelRef:      rcfg.ModelRef(),
91				Effort:        rcfg.Effort(),
92				Timeout:       rcfg.Timeout(),
93				WallClock:     rcfg.WallClock(),
94				WorkDir:       runtime.WorkDir,
95				Permissions:   rcfg.Permissions(),
96				SessionLogDir: sessionLogDir,
97			})
98		}

426	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
427	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
428	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
429	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
430	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
431		if configured == "" {

3	// dispatch.go owns the SD-024 dispatch seam shared by execute-bead and the
4	// post-merge reviewer. It threads durable knobs from a sealed
5	// config.ResolvedConfig with per-invocation plumbing/intent from an
6	// AgentRunRuntime through the same service-or-runner routing used by

9	import (

17	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
21	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.
23	//
24	// Resolution order:
25	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run after
26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
27	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.
30	//
31	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
32	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can pin one knob for
34	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
36		if runner != nil {
37			return runner.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
38		}
39		// DDx-local harnesses (virtual, script) are not upstream service harnesses.
40		// Route them through the local Runner so DDX_VIRTUAL_RESPONSES and script
41		// directives work correctly via compare and quorum paths, not just agent run.
42		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
43		if harness == "" {
44			harness = rcfg.Harness()
45		}
46		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
47			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, "")}
48			r := NewRunner(cfg)
49			r.WorkDir = projectRoot
50			return r.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
51		}
52		if svc == nil {
53			factory := serviceRunFactory
54			if factory == nil {
55				factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
56			}
57			built, err := factory(projectRoot)
58			if err != nil {
59				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
60			}
61			svc = built
62		}
63		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
64	}
65	
66	// buildRunArgsFromConfig assembles a RunArgs value for the test
67	// injection runner path. It applies AgentRunRuntime overrides so a caller
68	// (execute-bead worker, post-merge reviewer) can pin one durable knob for
69	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
70	func buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) RunArgs {
71		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
72		if harness == "" {
73			harness = rcfg.Harness()
74		}
75		model := runtime.ModelOverride
76		if model == "" {
77			model = rcfg.Model()
78		}
79		permissions := runtime.PermissionsOverride
80		if permissions == "" {
81			permissions = rcfg.Permissions()
82		}
83		sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
84		if sessionLogDir == "" {
85			sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
86		}
87	
88		var opts RunArgs
89		opts.Context = ctx
90		opts.Harness = harness
91		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="caller is responsible for bounding the prompt before invoking dispatchViaResolvedConfig; downstream executeOnService hits readPromptFileBounded for PromptFile inputs"
92		opts.Prompt = runtime.Prompt
93		opts.PromptFile = runtime.PromptFile
94		opts.PromptSource = runtime.PromptSource
95		opts.Correlation = runtime.Correlation
96		opts.Role = runtime.Role
97		opts.CorrelationID = runtime.CorrelationID
98		opts.Model = model
99		opts.Provider = rcfg.Provider()
100		opts.ModelRef = rcfg.ModelRef()
101		opts.Effort = rcfg.Effort()
102		opts.Timeout = rcfg.Timeout()
103		opts.WallClock = rcfg.WallClock()
104		opts.WorkDir = runtime.WorkDir
105		opts.Permissions = permissions
106		opts.SessionLogDir = sessionLogDir
107		return opts
108	}

76	// ResolveTriagePolicy returns the effective triage policy for this config,
77	// layering any `triage.policies` entries on top of the binary default
78	// ladders. Unknown failure-mode names and unknown action names are
79	// silently dropped (schema validation surfaces typos at load time).
80	func (c *NewConfig) ResolveTriagePolicy() triage.TriagePolicy {
81		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
82		if c == nil || c.Triage == nil || len(c.Triage.Policies) == 0 {
83			return policy
84		}
85		for modeStr, actionStrs := range c.Triage.Policies {
86			mode, err := triage.ParseFailureMode(modeStr)
87			if err != nil {
88				continue
89			}
90			ladder := make([]triage.Action, 0, len(actionStrs))
91			for _, a := range actionStrs {
92				act, err := triage.ParseAction(a)
93				if err != nil {
94					continue
95				}
96				ladder = append(ladder, act)
97			}
98			if len(ladder) == 0 {
99				continue
100			}
101			policy.Ladders[mode] = ladder
102		}
103		return policy
104	}

40	// appendProviderTimeoutHint appends a configuration override hint to errMsg
41	// when it indicates a provider request timeout fired. Helps operators find
42	// the knob to adjust (AC4 of ddx-2c63bb95).
43	func appendProviderTimeoutHint(errMsg string, providerTimeout time.Duration) string {
44		if errMsg == "" || !strings.Contains(errMsg, "provider request timeout") {
45			return errMsg
46		}
47		return errMsg + fmt.Sprintf(
48			"; request_timeout=%s exceeded — override via"+
49				" agent.endpoints.<name>.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml"+
50				" or pass --request-timeout DURATION to execute-bead/execute-loop",
51			providerTimeout.Round(time.Second),
52		)
53	}

55	// RunWithConfigViaService dispatches a single agent invocation through the
56	// agent service. It takes a sealed ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and an
57	// AgentRunRuntime (per-invocation plumbing/intent), and is the SD-024
58	// successor to the retired RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith entry points.
59	//
60	// The virtual and script harnesses route through a DDx-owned Runner path,
61	// not the upstream service. These are not "carve-outs pending migration" —
62	// they are different products from upstream's same-named stubs. DDx's
63	// virtual is a content-addressed record/replay dictionary keyed by
64	// PromptHash; upstream's is a unit-test stub where callers stuff
